text:
Epilepsy Centre 

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 16 November 2023 

Dr Patel  
St George's Hospital  
Department of Neurology, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Laura Bennett, DOB: 14-11-1989, Hospital No: H9374621 NHS No. 8426175309  
12 Larkspur Close, Wimbledon, London SW19 3AX  

Diagnoses: 

Focal epilepsy with presumed temporal lobe onset. 

Laura attended with her partner for routine review. She reports rare brief focal aware auras (metallic taste and déjà vu) occurring no more than once every 2–3 months without loss of awareness and with no secondary generalisation in over a year, reflecting very stable seizure control. There have been no emergency presentations and no rescue medication use. Known triggers include sleep deprivation; she is adhering to regular sleep and limiting alcohol. 

Current treatment: lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily, levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily. No adverse effects beyond occasional mild insomnia, which she manages with sleep hygiene. Neurological examination today was unremarkable. No device therapy in situ. 

Plan: continue current regimen unchanged; safety and SUDEP advice reinforced; driving guidance reviewed and unchanged given sustained seizure stability. Arrange routine bloods (U&E, LFTs) and lamotrigine level prior to next visit. Follow-up in 9 months, or sooner if breakthrough focal impaired awareness events occur. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: occurring no more than once every 2–3 months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: 'rare brief focal aware auras ... occurring no more than once every 2–3 months.' Focal aware auras are seizures, so the patient is not seizure-free. Another frequency is noted ('no secondary generalisation in over a year'), but per the rule to select the highest frequency, the focal aware seizure rate (1 per 2–3 months) is chosen. Normalize by converting the range with 'to' and keeping units singular.
seizure_frequency_number: occurring no more than once every 2–3 months

flaws.description: Gold (1 per 2 to 3 month) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
